---
title: "Data Summary"
author: "Hannah Farrell"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: 
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 4
    number_sections: true
    fig_caption: true
    highlight: tango
    latex_engine: xelatex
    keep_tex: true
fontsize: 12pt
geometry: landscape, margin=1in
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{float}

---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Data Summary 

```{r lib}
#Prepping the data for data summary
#Hannah 

#Prep Libraries 
remove(list = ls())

library(readr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(here)
library(stringr)


#Pull in the data
library(here)

options(scipen = 999)

df <- read.csv(here("MacroData_long_format_2 MAY 2026.csv"))
view(df)

#make the region names shorter 

df <- df %>%
  mutate(Region_clean = case_when(
    Region == "USA" ~ "USA",
    str_detect(Region, "Metro Area") ~ str_extract(Region, "[A-Z]{2}.*Metro Area"),
    TRUE ~ Region
  ))

```

```{r summary, echo=FALSE}
#summmary of dataframe
dim(df)        
nrow(df)
ncol(df)
colnames(df)
str(df)

#format better
df_info <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Number of Rows", "Number of Columns"),
  Value = c(nrow(df), ncol(df))
)

kable(df_info, caption = "Dataset Dimensions")

#summary_df <- data.frame(
#  Statistic = c("Min", "1st Quartile", "Median", "Mean", "3rd Quartile", "Max"),
#  Value = as.numeric(summary(df$Value)))

#kable(summary_df, caption = "Summary Statistics for Value")


#Filter out the non-MSA varibales 

filtered_df <- df %>%
  filter(
    Region != "USA",
    !Industry %in% c(
      "GDPC1",
      "GMP",
      "POP",
      "Total Employment",
      "Unemployment Rate"
    )
  )

# Create summary statistics table using filtered data
summary_filtered_df <- data.frame(
  Statistic = c("Min", "1st Quartile", "Median", "Mean", "3rd Quartile", "Max"),
  Value = as.numeric(summary(filtered_df$Value))
)

kable(summary_filtered_df, caption = "Summary Statistics for Employment Values (Thousands of People)")

```

```{r pressure}
#summary(df$Value)

###### Summary tables 

df %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    min = min(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    max = max(Value, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

filtered_df %>%
  filter(Region_clean != "USA") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Region_clean, y = Value)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Metro Region",
    y = "Thousands of People",
    title = "Distribution of Total Employment by Region"
  )

macro_df <- df %>%
  filter(
    Industry %in% c(
      "GDPC1",
      "GMP",
      "POP",
      "Total Employment",
      "Unemployment Rate"
    ))
```

```{r industry, echo=FALSE}
 summary_industry<- filtered_df %>%
  group_by(Industry) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    min = min(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    max = max(Value, na.rm = TRUE)
  )


 kable(summary_industry)
```

```{r macro, echo=FALSE}
 summary_macro<- macro_df %>%
  group_by(Industry) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    min = min(Value, na.rm = TRUE),
    max = max(Value, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
 
 kable(summary_macro)

```
Note: AI was used to utilize the kable formatting of the summary stats to be more atheistic  for the report. 

```{r excursion}
#excursion summmary of dataframe
excursion <- read.csv(here("fhfa_hpigrowth_full_clean(proxies)_2 MAY 2026.csv"))
view(excursion)
  
dim(excursion)        
nrow(excursion)
ncol(excursion)
colnames(excursion)
str(excursion)

#format better
excursion_info <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Number of Rows", "Number of Columns"),
  Value = c(nrow(excursion), ncol(excursion))
)

kable(excursion_info, caption = "Housing Price Index (HPI) Dataset Dimensions")

summary_excursion <- data.frame(
  Statistic = c("Min", "1st Quartile", "Median", "Mean", "3rd Quartile", "Max"),
  Value = as.numeric(summary(excursion$hpi)))


kable(summary_excursion, caption = "Summary Statistics for for Housing Price Index (HPI)")


#make the region names shorter 

excursion <- excursion %>%
  mutate(Region_clean = case_when(
    Region == "USA" ~ "USA",
    str_detect(Region, "Metro Area") ~ str_extract(Region, "[A-Z]{2}.*Metro Area"),
    TRUE ~ Region
  ))



##Charts 
excursion_index <- excursion %>%
  filter(Region != "USA") %>%
  group_by(Region) %>%
  arrange(Year) %>%
  mutate(index_value = hpi / hpi[Year == 2005] * 100) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(excursion_index, aes(x = Year, y = index_value, color = Region_clean)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 100, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  labs(
    title = "Metro Area Economic Trends (Indexed to 2005 = 100)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Index (2005 = 100)",
    color = "Metro Area"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")


```
